rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical fluorination-difunctionalization of gem-difluoroalkenes with CsF and nucleophiles (alcohols, azoles) to deliver trifluoromethylated/fluoroazolation products
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the gem-difluoroalkene substrate (oxidation onset 0.80 V vs SCE) initiates the transformation; fluoride/nucleophile addition and further oxidation generate cationic intermediates trapped by F- and nucleophile

key_intermediates.name: gem-difluoroalkene radical cation (from anodic SET on 1a)
benzyl radical (after fluoride addition to radical cation)
benzyl cation (after second anodic oxidation) trapped by nucleophile
oxidative gem-difluoroolefin intermediate (probed but not required)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the gem-difluoroalkene (1a) to a radical cation; fluoride (from CsF) adds to give a benzylic radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is trapped by the nucleophile (alcohol or azole) to install the second functional group.

probe_reagents.name: 4-vinylbiphenyl (5, normal alkene control)
probe_reagents.observation: No difluorination product 6 formed under standard conditions (2.0-3.5 V), indicating non-fluorinated alkenes are unreactive and the gem-difluoroalkene functionality is essential

probe_reagents.name: constant anode potential control (0.70 V and 0.90 V vs SCE on 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: At 0.70 V no product 2a formed; at 0.90 V 12% yield of 2a, supporting that anodic oxidation of the gem-difluoroalkene is required to initiate the reaction

probe_reagents.name: O2 (radical trapping under O2 atmosphere)
probe_reagents.observation: Ketone 7 (MW 250.2, ArC(O)CF3) detected by GC-MS, indicating a benzyl radical intermediate from 1a is involved

notes: Authors also probed whether an oxidative gem-difluoroolefin (overoxidized alkene) intermediate forms (Scheme 2b) — control experiments instead supported direct anodic oxidation of the gem-difluoroalkene. Full proposed pathway is given in Figure S3 (SI). A radiofluorination variant with K[18F]F-kryptofix was demonstrated (5% RCY), consistent with the same mechanism.
